OBJECTIONS TO DECL OF JOSEPH H. CABELLO FILED BY PLAINTIFF BOONE, ROGER M. A NATURAL PERSON

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JOSEPH CABALLERO'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY AS TO MOTION TO COMPEL AND MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS PENDING ARBITRATION. DEFENDANT HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN TO SHOW THE EXISTENCE OF THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT AND THAT THE DISPUTE IS COVERED BY THE AGREEMENT. THE PROMISSORY NOTE DOES NOT CONTAIN AN AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THE PARTIES EVER ORALLY AGREED TO ARBITRATE DISPUTES REGARDING THE PROMISSORY NOTE. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IN THE INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT AGREEMENTS DO NOT APPLY TO THE PROMISSORY NOTE. THE INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT AGREEMENTS ARE SIGNED BY TWO DIFFERENT TRUSTS, THE ROGER M. BOONE TRUST AND ROGER M. BOONE 2001 CHARITABLE REMAINDER UNITRUST. PLAINTIFF IS NOT A PARTY TO THOSE AGREEMENTS IN HIS INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY. THE ARBITRATION CLAUSES IN THE INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT AGREEMENTS ARE LIMITED TO THE SUBJECTS OF THOSE TWO AGREEMENTS. THE CLIENT INVESTMENT AGREEMENTS DO NOT CONTEMPLATE THE LOAN AGREEMENTS. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT BROUGHT AN ARBITRATION PROCEEDING AGAINST PERIGON PARTNERS LLC, PERIGON WEALTH MANAGEMENT, LLC, AND OTHER PERIGON OFFICERS BECAUSE OF PLAINTIFF'S COLLECTIONS EFFORTS, THIS DOES NOT MAKE PLAINTIFF A PARTY TO ARBITRATION BY AGREEMENT OR AS A THIRD PARTY THAT WOULD BE SUBJECT TO ARBITRATION, NOR DOES IT PROVIDE A BASIS TO STAY THESE PROCEEDINGS PENDING THAT ARBITRATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Ntc Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Apr-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MOHAMMAD WALIZADEH, ESQ., 415.995.5802, FOR PLAINTIFF ROGER BOON; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JOSEPH CABALLERO's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action is denied in its entirety as to motion to compel and motion to stay proceedings pending arbitration. Defendant has not met his burden to show the existence of the arbitration agreement and that the dispute is covered by the agreement. The promissory note does not contain an agreement to arbitrate. There is no evidence the parties ever orally agreed to arbitrate disputes regarding the promissory note. The arbitration agreement in the Investment Management Agreements do not apply to the promissory note. The Investment Management Agreements are signed by two different trusts, the Roger M. Boone Trust and Roger M. Boone 2001 Charitable Remainder Unitrust. Plaintiff is not a party to those agreements in his individual capacity. The arbitration clauses in the Investment Management Agreements are limited to the subjects of those two agreements. The client investment agreements do not contemplate the loan agreements. Although Defendant brought an arbitration proceeding against Perigon Partners LLC, Perigon Wealth Management, LLC, and other Perigon officers because of Plaintiff's collections efforts, this does not make Plaintiff a party to arbitration by agreement or as a third party that would be subject to arbitration, nor does it provide a basis to stay these proceedings pending that arbitration. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
